mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises gradually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance at p greater than 0.10 in multivariate regression or positive return frequency drops below 45% when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 240 while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent, markets price in delayed monetary tightening and stagflation risks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation low-rate periods or inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which correlates with higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or positive return frequency does not exceed 50% during production expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50 thousand signals abrupt labor market contraction and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of sharp payroll decline months or signal fails to predict returns when declines are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand growth, reinforcing earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of co-expansion quarters or interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in greater than 60% of declining production months during late-cycle phases or signal loses edge when inventory adjustments are excluded.
